title: white-hacker project-scope companion profile
description: A GATED, project-scope companion the generic white-hacker agent consumes. It specializes detection-derived facts ONLY. additionalProperties:false at the top level GUARANTEES no shipped-identity key (posture, tools, tool-scope, output-contract, review-stages) can ever appear — init never rewrites the shipped identity (ADR-004, ADR-017).

generated_note.description: A factual one-line provenance statement (never an imperative instruction).

present_capabilities.description: Security capabilities backed by an installed tool (sast/sca/secrets/iac/ai-redteam).

tools_unavailable.description: Capabilities with no installed tool; the agent degrades these to the Read/Grep/Glob floor.

ai_pass.description: True when LLM/AI deps are present; the AI/LLM review pass applies.

package_managers.description: Package managers detected from manifests + lockfiles (npm/pnpm/yarn/bun, uv/poetry/pip, go, maven/gradle). Factual identifiers the user confirms/corrects during onboarding. Additive (NOT required) — old profiles without it still validate.

build_test_commands.description: Best-guess build/test/lint/run commands the user confirms. A FIXED key set with additionalProperties:false — NOT a free-form map — so a manifest-influenced value can never smuggle an unknown command role (the stricter option vs patternProperties; preserves the structural identity guarantee of ADR-004/017). Values are FACTUAL command strings (e.g. 'uv run pytest'); write_profile's is_factual gate rejects imperatives.

in_scope_focus.description: OPTIONAL project-specific concerns the user names (e.g. 'payment flow', 'auth token handling'). Factual noun phrases — write_profile's is_factual gate rejects imperative phrasing.

scoring_standard.description: Severity scoring standard (e.g. CVSS or an org bug-bar). Defaults to null: must be human-confirmed, never hardcoded.

security_policy.description: Coordinated-disclosure policy facts detected by sec-init (SECURITY.md and/or an RFC 9116 security.txt). Structural facts only — booleans, a closed reporting-channel enum, and the repo-relative path; never any file-body span (the file is untrusted data).

present.description: True when a SECURITY.md exists at one of the known locations.

path.description: Repo-relative path of the detected SECURITY.md, or null when absent.

reporting_channel.description: How vulnerabilities are reported: GitHub private vulnerability reporting, an email, a generic URL, none, or unknown.

supported_versions_present.description: True when the policy has a 'Supported Versions' section.

disclosure_timeline_present.description: True when the policy states a numeric coordinated-disclosure window.

security_txt_present.description: True when an RFC 9116 security.txt exists.

security_txt_expired.description: True if the security.txt Expires field is in the past, False if valid, null when absent/unparseable.
